title: exoacantha heterophylla
intro: Exoacantha heterophylla, also known as the Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow trumpet-shaped flowers and distinctive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Exoacantha heterophylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, making it a popular herbal remedy. Exoacantha heterophylla is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
